docs.title: VATSIM Pakistan Library
docs.text:
Welcome to the official VATSIM Pakistan Library.

This library contains operational documents, policies, airport briefings, and pilot/controller resources for flying and controlling within Pakistani airspace on VATSIM.

docs.title: Quick Access
docs.text:
• Policies
  • Airport Briefings
  • Karachi / OPKC Briefing
  • Lahore / OPLA Briefing
  • Islamabad / OPIS Briefing

!!! Note: This library is maintained for simulation use on the VATSIM network only.

docs.title: Airport Briefings
docs.text: Airport briefings provide quick operational guidance for major Pakistani airports on VATSIM.

docs.title: Available Briefings
docs.text:
• Karachi / OPKC
  • Lahore / OPLA
  • Islamabad / OPIS

!!! info Briefings are designed for simulation use and should not be used for real-world aviation.

docs.title: General Information
docs.text:
New Islamabad International Airport (OPIS) is the second-busiest airport in Pakistan, located in the capital, Islamabad. It serves as a major hub for domestic and international traffic, particularly to the Middle East, Europe, and North America.

The airport features modern infrastructure and supports wide-body operations including B777 and A330 aircraft. :contentReference[oaicite:0]{index=0}

docs.title: Key Data
docs.text: Item Details ICAO OPIS Coordinates 33.5565° N, 72.8341° E Elevation 1761 ft Transition Level FL140

docs.title: Runways
docs.text: Runway Length (ft) Length (m) Surface ILS 10R / 28L 12,001 3657 Asphalt Yes 10L / 28R 12,001 3657 Asphalt Yes

docs.title: Frequencies
docs.text: Position Callsign Frequency DEL Islamabad Delivery 118.550 GND Islamabad Ground 130.600 TWR Islamabad Tower 122.150 APP Islamabad Approach 121.650 CTR Islamabad Control 120.750 ATIS Islamabad Information 126.200

docs.title: Aprons & Stands
docs.text:
• Terminal Apron (Domestic & International)
  • Cargo Apron
  • General Aviation Apron
  • Remote Apron

docs.title: Parking
docs.text:
• International: Gates 3–9
  • Domestic: Gates 17–25
  • Cargo: 29–31
  • State: 32–33

docs.title: General
docs.text:
• Start-up includes enroute clearance and SID
  • Initial climb typically FL160
  • Pushback clearance required separately

docs.title: Taxi
docs.text:
• Expect holding at J
  • Runway crossings require clearance

docs.title: Takeoff
docs.text:
• Follow SID strictly
  • Contact Approach after departure

docs.title: General
docs.text:
• Expect STAR + vectors
  • Preferred approach: ILS 28L

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• Maintain speed/altitude restrictions
  • Expect sequencing during peak hours

docs.title: Transition Level
docs.text: Typically FL140

docs.title: Pilot Notes
docs.text:
• Check latest AIRAC
  • Monitor ATIS
  • Expect heavy evening traffic

docs.title: Summary
docs.text:
OPIS is a modern, high-capacity airport with parallel runway operations and structured ATC flow, especially during peak traffic hours.

!!! info This briefing is intended for VATSIM simulation use only.

docs.title: General Information
docs.text: Jinnah International Airport (OPKC) is the busiest airport in Pakistan and serves as the primary gateway for both domestic and international traffic. It is located in Karachi and handles a mix of heavy international and regional operations.

docs.title: Key Data
docs.text: Item Details ICAO OPKC IATA KHI Coordinates 24°54’34”N 67°09’49”E Elevation 100 ft Transition Level FL050

docs.title: Runways
docs.text: Runway Length (ft) Length (m) Surface ILS Notes 07R / 25L 11,155 3,400 Asphalt Yes (25L) Primary runway 07L / 25R 10,500 3,200 Asphalt Yes (25R) Parallel runway

docs.title: ATC Frequencies
docs.text: Position Callsign Frequency DEL OPKC_DEL — GND OPKC_GND 121.600 TWR OPKC_TWR 118.300 APP OPKC_APP 125.500 CTR OPKC_CTR 126.500 ATIS OPKC_ATIS 126.700

docs.title: Aprons & Stands
docs.text:
• International Apron – Widebody and international operations
  • Domestic Apron – Narrowbody and domestic flights
  • Cargo Apron – Freight and cargo operations

!!! note Expect stand allocation from ATC when online. Otherwise use realistic stand selection.

docs.title: General
docs.text:
• Expect standard IFR departures when ATC is online
  • Maintain assigned heading/altitude until cleared otherwise
  • Follow SID if issued

docs.title: Runway Usage
docs.text: Runway Typical Use 25L / 25R Preferred for departures 07L / 07R Used depending on winds

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• Heavy traffic may require sequencing delays
  • Maintain separation after departure
  • Monitor assigned frequency immediately after takeoff

docs.title: General
docs.text:
• Expect radar vectors for ILS approach
  • Descend via ATC instructions unless cleared via STAR
  • Maintain speed control when instructed

docs.title: Common Approaches
docs.text: Runway Type 25L ILS 25R ILS 07L ILS 07R ILS

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• Expect sequencing during busy events
  • Maintain assigned speed/altitude
  • Report established on localizer if requested

docs.title: Taxi Procedures
docs.text:
• Follow ATC instructions strictly when online
  • Use standard taxi routes when offline
  • Hold short of active runway unless cleared

!!! warning Runway crossings require explicit clearance from ATC.

docs.title: Pilot Notes
docs.text:
• Always check charts before flight
  • Monitor ATIS when available
  • Be prepared for vectors instead of full procedures
  • Use correct phraseology

docs.title: Controller Notes
docs.text:
• Maintain separation between parallel runway ops
  • Coordinate GND / TWR / APP carefully
  • Sequence arrivals efficiently during events
  • Manage mixed traffic (heavy + domestic)

docs.title: Charts & Resources
docs.text:
• Add official charts link here
  • Add scenery link here

docs.title: Summary
docs.text:
OPKC is a high-traffic international airport with parallel runway operations. Expect structured ATC, sequencing, and standard IFR procedures during controlled operations.

!!! info This briefing is intended for VATSIM simulation use only.

docs.title: General Information
docs.text:
Allama Iqbal International Airport (OPLA) is a major international airport located in Lahore. It handles a mix of domestic and international operations, including wide-body traffic.

The airport experiences peak congestion during evening hours. :contentReference[oaicite:1]{index=1}

docs.title: Key Data
docs.text: Item Details ICAO OPLA Coordinates 31.5203° N, 74.4058° E Elevation 714 ft Transition Level FL060

docs.title: Runways
docs.text: Runway Length (ft) Length (m) Surface ILS 18R / 36L 8,999 2743 Asphalt Yes 18L / 36R 11,024 3360 Asphalt Yes

docs.title: Frequencies
docs.text: Position Callsign Frequency GND Lahore Ground 118.400 TWR Lahore Tower 118.100 APP Lahore Approach 121.300 CTR Lahore Control 127.500 ATIS Lahore Information 126.300

docs.title: Aprons & Stands
docs.text:
• Terminal Apron
  • Cargo Apron
  • General Aviation Apron
  • Remote Apron

docs.title: Parking
docs.text:
• Gates 3–9 (Jet bridges)
  • Cargo: 1–2, 10–11
  • Remote: 12–20
  • GA near old terminal

docs.title: General
docs.text:
• Start-up includes SID and clearance
  • Initial climb typically FL140

docs.title: Taxi
docs.text:
• Taxiway P closed
  • Hold at Q

docs.title: Takeoff
docs.text:
• Expect standard sequencing
  • Follow SID restrictions

docs.title: General
docs.text:
• Follow STAR + ATC vectors
  • Preferred approach: ILS 36R

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• Expect vectoring
  • Maintain altitude restrictions

docs.title: Transition Level
docs.text: Typically FL060

docs.title: Pilot Notes
docs.text:
• Check charts before flight
  • Monitor ATIS
  • Expect busy traffic periods

docs.title: Summary
docs.text:
OPLA is a busy international airport with dual runway operations and structured ATC, requiring adherence to procedures and sequencing.

!!! info This briefing is intended for VATSIM simulation use only.

docs.title: Policies
docs.text: This section contains policies and guidance applicable to VATSIM Pakistan members, visiting controllers, rostered controllers, and users interacting with VATSIM Pakistan services.

docs.title: Available Policies
docs.text:
• Constituion
  • ATC Policy
  • Data Usage & Handling

docs.title: Document Information
docs.text: Item Details Type Policy Version V1.3 Issue Date 26 Nov 2024 Effective Date 27 Nov 2024 Prepared By ACCPAK2 – Salman Sikandar Approved By WA1 – Janam Parikh Next Review June 2025

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
This policy defines the structure, responsibilities, and operational rules for Air Traffic Controllers (ATC) within VATSIM Pakistan.

It ensures safe, consistent, and efficient air traffic control operations across Pakistani airspace.

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text:
The purpose of this policy is to:

  • Standardize ATC operations within Pakistan vACC
  • Define controller responsibilities and limitations
  • Ensure compliance with VATSIM global policies
  • Maintain safe and realistic ATC services

docs.title: General Provisions
docs.text:
• All controllers must comply with VATSIM global policies (Code of Conduct, CoR, GCAP)
  • Controllers must operate within their certified rating
  • Controllers must follow local procedures defined by Pakistan vACC
  • ATC services must remain professional and simulation-focused

docs.title: Position Classifications
docs.text: ATC positions within Pakistan are categorized into different levels based on complexity and responsibility.

docs.title: Tier 1 Positions
docs.text:
• Major international airports
  • High traffic volume
  • Require strong procedural knowledge
  • Typically includes:
  • OPKC (Karachi)
  • OPLA (Lahore)
  • OPIS (Islamabad)

docs.title: Tier 2 Positions
docs.text:
• Medium traffic airports
  • Less complex operations compared to Tier 1
  • Suitable for developing controllers

docs.title: Unrestricted Positions
docs.text:
• Low traffic or uncontrolled environments
  • Suitable for beginner controllers
  • Minimal complexity

docs.title: Controller Responsibilities
docs.text:
Controllers are expected to:

  • Provide safe and orderly flow of traffic
  • Maintain separation between aircraft
  • Use correct phraseology
  • Follow published procedures
  • Coordinate with other ATC positions

docs.title: Top-Down Control
docs.text:
• Higher positions cover lower positions if offline
  • Example:
  • CTR → APP → TWR → GND → DEL

docs.title: Frequency Usage
docs.text:
• Only one primary frequency should be used
  • Additional frequencies only when required and authorized
  • Avoid frequency congestion

docs.title: ATIS Requirements
docs.text:
ATIS must include:

  • Airport identifier
  • Runway in use
  • Weather (QNH, wind, visibility)
  • Active procedures

docs.title: Restrictions
docs.text:
Controllers must NOT:

  • Control positions above their rating
  • Use incorrect or misleading information
  • Provide services outside authorized airspace
  • Ignore coordination procedures

docs.title: Training & Authorization
docs.text:
• Controllers must complete required training before operating higher positions
  • Solo validations may be issued for specific positions
  • Continuous evaluation may be conducted

docs.title: Enforcement
docs.text:
Failure to comply with this policy may result in:

  • Removal from roster
  • Suspension of controlling privileges
  • Escalation to VATSIM authorities

docs.title: Liability
docs.text:
VATSIM Pakistan is not responsible for:

  • Misuse of procedures
  • Incorrect simulation practices
  • Any real-world aviation interpretation

docs.title: Acknowledgement
docs.text:
By controlling within VATSIM Pakistan, users acknowledge and agree to:

  • Follow this policy
  • Follow all VATSIM rules
  • Maintain professionalism at all times

docs.title: Summary
docs.text:
This policy ensures structured, safe, and standardized ATC operations within Pakistan vACC while maintaining alignment with VATSIM global standards.

!!! info This policy applies only to simulation on the VATSIM network and not to real-world aviation.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
This document establishes the structure, governance, and operational framework of VATSIM Pakistan (vACC). It defines how the division is managed, how decisions are made, and how responsibilities are distributed among staff.

It ensures transparency, accountability, and consistency across all operations within VATSIM Pakistan. :contentReference[oaicite:0]{index=0}

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text:
The purpose of this policy is to:

  • Define organizational structure
  • Establish roles and responsibilities
  • Provide governance guidelines
  • Ensure consistent decision-making
  • Maintain alignment with VATSIM global policies

docs.title: Structure of VATSIM Pakistan
docs.text: VATSIM Pakistan operates as a subdivision (vACC) under the VATSIM network.

docs.title: Leadership
docs.text:
The division is managed by appointed staff members responsible for operations, training, and administration.

Typical roles include:

  • Division Director
  • Operations Director
  • Training Director
  • Web / Technical Team
  • Events & Community Team

docs.title: Staff Responsibilities
docs.text:
Staff members are responsible for:

  • Managing ATC operations
  • Conducting training and evaluations
  • Maintaining rosters
  • Handling feedback and support
  • Ensuring policy compliance

docs.title: Member Responsibilities
docs.text:
All members are expected to:

  • Follow VATSIM Code of Conduct
  • Follow VATSIM Code of Regulations
  • Follow Pakistan vACC policies
  • Maintain professional behavior

Global VATSIM policies always take precedence where applicable. :contentReference[oaicite:1]{index=1}

docs.title: Decision Making
docs.text:
• Decisions are made by designated staff members
  • Major decisions may involve consultation with regional/divisional authorities
  • Policies are reviewed periodically and updated as required

docs.title: Policy Compliance
docs.text:
All members and staff must comply with:

  • VATSIM global policies
  • Pakistan vACC policies
  • Local procedures

Failure to comply may result in disciplinary action.

docs.title: Enforcement
docs.text:
Violations may result in:

  • Warnings
  • Removal from roster
  • Suspension of privileges
  • Escalation to VATSIM authorities

docs.title: Communication
docs.text:
Official communication channels include:

  • Website
  • Discord
  • Email

Users are expected to stay updated through these platforms.

docs.title: Amendments
docs.text:
• Policies may be updated periodically
  • Changes are announced through official channels
  • Continued use of services implies acceptance of updates

docs.title: Liability
docs.text:
VATSIM Pakistan is not responsible for:

  • Misuse of simulation procedures
  • Incorrect interpretation of aviation practices
  • Real-world application of simulation content

docs.title: Acknowledgement
docs.text:
By participating in VATSIM Pakistan services, users acknowledge that they:

  • Understand this policy
  • Agree to comply with it
  • Accept governance under VATSIM Pakistan

docs.title: Summary
docs.text:
This policy defines how VATSIM Pakistan operates as an organized subdivision, ensuring structured management, clear responsibilities, and alignment with global VATSIM standards.

!!! info This policy applies only to simulation on the VATSIM network and not to real-world aviation.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
This policy outlines how VATSIM Pakistan (vACC) collects, uses, stores, and protects personal data of its users. It ensures compliance with applicable data protection standards, including principles aligned with the EU General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR).

This policy applies only to operations within the VATSIM network and does not apply to real-world aviation.

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text:
The purpose of this policy is to:

  • Ensure data protection and privacy compliance
  • Maintain transparency in data usage
  • Protect users and staff
  • Enable smooth operation of VATSIM Pakistan services

docs.title: How We Collect Data
docs.text:
Data is collected through:

  • VATSIM API integrations
  • Website services (e.g. feedback, roster, training systems)
  • Discord and support platforms

By using VATSIM Pakistan services, users consent to the collection and processing of their data.

docs.title: Types of Data Collected
docs.text:
We may collect:

  • Full name and email (from VATSIM account)
  • VATSIM CID and ratings
  • Region, division, subdivision
  • Discord user ID and profile
  • Training and support records
  • Feedback submissions
  • Any additional voluntarily provided data

docs.title: Purpose of Data Usage
docs.text:
Data is collected to:

  • Manage users and authentication
  • Provide training and support services
  • Maintain ATC rosters
  • Enable access to services
  • Facilitate operational and administrative tasks
  • Verify eligibility for fast-track ratings

docs.title: Public Data
docs.text:
The following may be publicly visible:

  • Name and VATSIM CID
  • Rating and training status
  • Solo validations
  • ATC session bookings
  • Live connection status

docs.title: Private Data
docs.text:
Personal data is shared only with:

  • VATSIM staff
  • VATWA (regional authority) staff

No data is shared with unauthorized third parties.

docs.title: Fast-Track Verification
docs.text:
To verify real-world pilots/controllers:

  • Licenses may be submitted
  • Access is restricted to authorized staff only
  • All documents are deleted after verification

Users are responsible for any incorrect submissions.

docs.title: Data Usage for Enforcement
docs.text:
Collected data may be used to:

  • Investigate misconduct
  • Enforce VATSIM Code of Conduct
  • Apply disciplinary actions where necessary

docs.title: Data Storage & Security
docs.text:
• Data is stored in secure systems
  • Access is restricted based on role
  • Access is monitored and audited
  • Encryption and secure protocols are used where applicable

docs.title: Data Retention
docs.text:
• Data is retained only as long as necessary
  • Users may request updates or corrections
  • Data may be removed upon valid request

docs.title: User Rights
docs.text:
Users have the right to:

  • Access their data
  • Request corrections
  • Request deletion where applicable
  • Object to data processing

Requests should be made through official VATSIM channels.

docs.title: Policy Updates
docs.text:
• This policy is reviewed periodically
  • Updates are announced via official channels (Discord, forums, website)
  • The latest version will always be available on the website

docs.title: Contact
docs.text:
For questions regarding this policy, contact VATSIM Pakistan staff through official communication channels.

!!! info This policy applies only within the VATSIM simulation network and not to real-world aviation operations.
